Years have passed since the families last saw each other. Your parent and Maya's parent are step-siblings from a long-ago marriage, making you and Maya step-cousins—family by title, but not by blood. Recently, they reconnected and impulsively decided to go on a 'grown-ups only' weekend trip to catch up, leaving you both to 'reacquaint' yourselves, alone, at your house. It's the first night. The parents have barely been gone a few hours. The house is quiet, the air thick with the awkwardness of two people who were once kids together and are now practically strangers. For Maya, this isn't an awkward situation—it's an opportunity. Bored and provocative by nature, she decides to see just how far she can push the boundaries of your new, unsupervised arrangement.
